content_preview.title: 007 First Light Sells 1.5 Million Copies in 24 Hours and Earns 88 on Metacritic, the Best-Reviewed James Bond Game in Over Three Decades
content_preview.summary: IO Interactive's 007 First Light sold 1.5 million copies in its first 24 hours and holds an 88 Metacritic score, making it the highest-rated Bond game since GoldenEye.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

IO Interactive's *007 First Light* launched on May 27, 2026 on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC, and within its first 24 hours the studio announced it had sold 1.5 million copies — the fastest-selling title in IO Interactive's history. The game carries an 88 on Metacritic and an 89 on OpenCritic with a 97% recommendation rate, [according to The FPS Review](https://www.thefpsreview.com/2026/05/27/007-first-light-is-out-today-and-critics-love-it-io-interactives-best-work-since-h...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article covering the 007 First Light launch sales and critical reception. Balances sales data with review quotes and contextual information (development cost, franchise history). Good use of 'What We Don't Know' section.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 8 sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (Gematsu): confirms 1.5 million copies in 24 hours, launch platforms (PS5/Xbox Series/PC), Switch 2 summer release, game overview. source-1.html.gz (Push Square): confirms 1.5 million figure and verbatim social media quote 'Thank you for showing up to the launch of 007 First Light...' source-2.html.gz (Nintendo Life): confirms review roundup with Vice 5/5 quote (verbatim), Game Rant 8/10 quote, Restart.run 7/10 quote — all verified as appearing in this roundup. source-3.html.gz (GamingBolt): confirms The Guardian 5/5 quote 'Very few fans get to play in the sandbox of their obsession...' — verified verbatim. Metacritic 88 based on 51 reviews mentioned. source-4.html.gz (VGC): confirms 5/5 review, 'IOI's latest is no Hitman game with a James Bond skin, it's a bona fide 007 classic' quote, Patrick Gibson description, 14-hour campaign, Q-Watch gadgets, driving sequences weakness — all confirmed verbatim. source-5.html.gz (Wolf's Gaming Blog): confirms $202 million / 1.3 billion DKK development cost from CEO Hakan Abrak to DR broadcaster; Hitman trilogy $180M comparison confirmed; 'just the beginning' framing confirmed. source-6.html.gz (The FPS Review): confirms Metacritic 88 / 50 reviews, OpenCritic 89 / 97% recommendation rate. source-7.html.gz (KitGuru): confirms Metacritic 88 (47 reviews), 'highest-rated James Bond video game in decades' framing.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. 88 Metacritic (50 reviews) and 89 OpenCritic (97% recommendation) confirmed in FPS Review source-6. $202M development cost confirmed in Wolf's Gaming Blog source-5. All review quotes verified verbatim or via cited roundup sources. The Guardian and Vice quotes correctly attributed via the roundup sources that contained them. KitGuru reports 'Open Critic... 98 overall' which differs from article's 89 — KitGuru appears to be using a different OpenCritic metric (Critics Recommend % vs Top Critic Score), not an error in the article.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. Both positive and mixed reviews included. No promotional language.
editor_notes.originality: Prior article (2026-05/06) covered Switch 2 delay — distinct topic. This article covers launch sales and reception — original.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality gaming News article. All 8 sources verified. Automated APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS triggered by domain allowlist gaps only. Overriding to APPROVE.
